prompts.image:
[14] IMAGE (motion start frame)
STYLE SYSTEM (the world rendering law — this is HOW to render, not WHAT; apply every clause below to the single concrete frame briefed next): Fortiche painterly-3D hybrid rendering as developed by Fortiche Production for premium episodic animation. This is a precise formal system, not 'anime-ish' — the grammar is industrial European painterly, not Japanese: (1) FORMS: full 3D geometry, but surface shading is a painted oil-texture layer — brush strokes are visible at every silhouette edge, their direction following the form's curvature but inconsistently, as if a painter made deliberate choices rather than a renderer solving physics. (2) COLOUR LAW: there is no black anywhere in this world. The deepest shadow is a rich deep violet. Skin shadow is rosewood-plum, architectural shadow is indigo-violet, cloth shadow is aubergine. (3) HIGHLIGHTS: painted-in manually, NOT following physical light math exactly — highlight placement emphasizes the emotional reading of the form (the cheekbone lit even when key is behind the subject, the knuckle catching a gleam on the swing). (4) SURFACE SPECIFICITY: skin has painted-in blush and pore mark visible at close-up, not subsurface scattering. Hair reads as sculpted painted volume — individual strands are grouped into painted clumps, each clump receiving a single highlight stroke. Fabric shows woven texture painted, not cloth-sim. (5) ENVIRONMENT: chunky mid-poly architecture with surface painting of grime strata, rust oxidation pattern, and iridescent oil-slick streaks on wet stone — a deep-industrial undercity aesthetic of chemical-district grime, never any named franchise district. Every metal surface carries thin-film interference rainbow spec. Background compresses into painterly matte with visible brushwork — never crisp photographic detail, never clean CGI surface. (6) POST: Vision3 500T film stock emulation — warm-highlight / cool-shadow curve, halation blooming on any pixel above 90% luminance (light sources bloom into surrounding haze), subtle chromatic aberration at high-contrast silhouette edges, 35mm-equivalent grain floor present but refined. IMPERATIVE: FORTICHE PAINTERLY-3D HYBRID. 3D forms with hand-painted surface shading, 2D-animated FX layer on top. FORBID: flat cel shading, clean untextured CGI, photoreal live-action light math, standard anime eye geometry, pure black. Line grammar: No hard cel outline anywhere. Silhouette carries entirely through painted edge — the brush stroke itself IS the boundary, weight varies from 2px at low emotional intensity to 5px at high, direction follows the form. Interior 'lines' are painted shadow steps: a steep value jump that reads as line at distance but resolves to brushwork at close. Shadow-side rim is painted deep violet, not inked. Effects layer (energy, fire, sparks) has its own distinct loose calligraphic brush contour that contrasts with the controlled character silhouette. Lens grammar: 35mm to 50mm portrait range. f/2.8 character close-up, f/4 mid-shot, f/5.6 wide establisher. Vision3 500T characteristic curve: warm highlight (+15 orange shift above 70% luminance), cool shadow (cyan push below 30%), halation blooming on anything above 90% luminance. 35mm grain floor present and refined. Mild chromatic aberration (1-2px lateral shift at silhouette). No anamorphic streak. 1.78:1 or 2.00:1 aspect. Light law: Single motivated key direction — warm industrial lantern, cool exterior moon, neon-adjacent accent from a painted window. Bounce fill is in the key's complementary hue from the palette: magenta key bounces teal fill, teal key bounces magenta fill. Rim accent color is always from the palette's jewel-tone (never white, never warm neutral). Ambient occlusion is painted soft and warm-dark, never a hard black AO contact shadow. Halation blooms on all practical sources in frame. Painted indirect-light from windows and lamps bleeds warm halos onto proximate geometry. Scene brief (Claude yazar): "Arada sırada bu tavernaya gelip birasını yudumlar ya da tepedeki bilge ile konuşurdu."" [SOURCE — do not render as on-screen text; narration only]. [DIRECTOR TASK — authored by Claude, not image content, do not print into the frame: compose ONE concrete single-frame scene faithful to this world's grammar above; author the dominant element and its half-second motion seed from the source.] Staging: logical hierarchy: components spatially ranked by importance, reading line clear, negative space guides the eye; strict composition — centered or negative-space led, frame edges intentional. Mood law: warm and tender - gentle unhurried pacing, soft warm palette, intimate close staging, emotional music motif. Reference anchor (subordinate to the world rendering law above — it colours HOW the frame is made, it never overrides the world, the palette or the negative lock): Fortiche painterly-3D: oil-paint brush stroke at every silhouette, deep-violet shadow (no black), teal+magenta two-color bounce, Vision3 500T halation, smear frames painted not motion-blurred; Fortiche undercity value-tension: painted-3D grime and oil-slick iridescence on wet industrial stone, deep-violet shadow (no black), teal-magenta rim system, thin-film metal spec, painterly smear frames, value-charged hold; Fortiche bridge-scale: monumental ornate painted-3D span, figure small against vast structure, warm-stone-and-gilt ornament against cool atmospheric sky, deep-violet shadow, painterly depth, held awe-of-scale. Camera grammar (this world's framing law — it governs the vantage; the move below happens inside it): 35-50mm portrait staging with a painted 3D weight: the character close-up carved out of a deep, softly falling background. The camera commits to one bold, deliberate move — a slow push onto the decisive gesture, a lateral track through the layers — then holds while the paint settles. Frame at the character's own height; the world is dense around them, never above them. Never a handheld shake, never a whip pan, never a top-down vantage. Camera/vantage: locked low-pressure angle held inside the frame, negative space carrying the wait. Composition pattern: rising diagonal — the dominant element enters on the lower-left third, its action line pointing toward upper-right light. Camera energy: slow location dolly or slider - reveal real space, practical depth and human-scale anchors before the hero beat. POV rule: witness POV — observe a real person or place without forcing performance; credibility over spectacle — only where it reveals the idea; a locked frame is valid. Light: hard value separation: one strong key, deep readable shadow shapes; warm motivated key with a named source (window, lamp, low sun). Light variant: motivate the key from the opposite side and let the shadow mass lead the composition. Time-of-day mandate: motivated window naturalism - believable direction, skin-safe falloff, room air and honest contrast. Palette physics: Palette light: near-black cool indigo, bright cool teal, vivid warm red, near-white warm ivory — palette character: deep-violet shadow + jewel-teal mid + magenta-pink accent + warm-cream highlight. Thin-film rainbow spec on all metal. Grime palette: rust-orange, oxidized copper, oil-slick iridescent (context-dependent). Render these as light behaviour, never flat fills. Texture rule: exactly ONE texture clause per prompt, from the "painterly" family — texture is seasoning, never the subject. Director mandate: Phase 0 preset: Sinematik Hikâye. Director thesis: Duyguyu reklam parlatmasıyla değil, gerçek mekan ve sinema grameriyle taşı. Text/logo: clean plate — this scene carries no on-screen text. No floating text, no caption, no subtitle, no watermark, no added signage; the image alone carries the meaning and the narration speaks it. Any Turkish text or logo already native to the scene is frozen geometry — only light and camera may cross it. Path contract (outranks the world grammar above when they disagree): the frame MUST deliver iP-safe style grammar: texture, light, value, camera, staging. Negative: copied characters, logos, exact shots; NO shimmer substance (iridescent purple glow serum); NO pure black anywhere in the frame — deepest shadow is deep violet; NO hard cel outline, NO standard anime flat-color shading; NO earth green, NO pure orange (flame accent excepted), NO sepia-brown palette; NOT anime; NO English signage; no recognizable franchise or real-person characters, logos, brand names; the named thing replaced by a symbol for it — no icon, pictogram, chart, diagram, arrow, gauge or infographic panel standing in for the real object or the real action; the literal thing is IN the frame, photographed/rendered as matter, not illustrated as a concept; empty adjectives (cinematic, dynamic, stunning, 4K); flat slide; warped or drifting text; character retyping. Clean motion-ready start frame.
prompts.motionDraft:
[14] MOTION (i2v · plays the approved start frame)
Camera: locked low-pressure angle held inside the frame, negative space carrying the wait. Motion brief (Claude yazar): source beat "Arada sırada bu tavernaya gelip birasını yudumlar ya da tepedeki bilge ile konuşurdu."" [SOURCE — do not render as on-screen text; narration only]. [DIRECTOR TASK — authored by Claude against the approved start frame, not clip content, do not print into the clip: compose ONE single-action, frame-aware motion faithful to the source; only the dominant element already in the frame moves — no new object or scenery enters.] Rhythm: weighted colour settle: motion ends with a material sense of gravity, warm/cold shift confirms the beat; spatial drift: camera or light drifts through one threshold, lands and holds in the destination quality — paced by the engine law: attack lands inside the first second, the event resolves by ~70% of the clip, the rest is held; everything settles naturally into a stable 1-1.5s final hold. Motion cadence (this world's own motion signature — the authority for HOW this world moves; obey its cadence discipline exactly, but where it names a hold duration the engine law's final-hold timing above supersedes): Deliberate 24fps with hand-animated painted smear frames at peak action — 2-3 consecutive frames with painted directional motion streak visible as brushwork, not motion-blur. 1.5-2 second hold on emotional beat — character studies the situation, eyes tracking. Slow-in / slow-out weight. Momentum carries: a punch's aftershock ripples through cloth and hair for 4-6 frames after impact. No motion blur — smears are painted. Engine grammar (Kling 3.0): Kling 3.0 start-frame fidelity: the approved frame is truth — describe only what changes, never re-describe what already exists; the motion reads as the frame's next half-second. Everything not named stays exactly as the start frame shows: world, material, light, faces, text, logo, geometry — never re-described, never re-rendered.
NEGATIVE: morphing, warping, re-render, style or material drift, new objects or scenery, leaving the frame, face or identity change, mouth movement, logo/text/geometry change, multiple actions, flicker, re-render of the start frame.
[DIRECTOR TASK — frame-specific negatives, do not print into the clip: before finalizing, specialize the baseline negative above to THIS approved frame — name its actual fragile elements (the exact text plate, thin rigging/lines, a reflection edge, the logo) in place of the generic list.]

draft.previewPrompt:
[14] IMAGE (motion start frame)
STYLE SYSTEM (the world rendering law — this is HOW to render, not WHAT; apply every clause below to the single concrete frame briefed next): Fortiche painterly-3D hybrid rendering as developed by Fortiche Production for premium episodic animation. This is a precise formal system, not 'anime-ish' — the grammar is industrial European painterly, not Japanese: (1) FORMS: full 3D geometry, but surface shading is a painted oil-texture layer — brush strokes are visible at every silhouette edge, their direction following the form's curvature but inconsistently, as if a painter made deliberate choices rather than a renderer solving physics. (2) COLOUR LAW: there is no black anywhere in this world. The deepest shadow is a rich deep violet. Skin shadow is rosewood-plum, architectural shadow is indigo-violet, cloth shadow is aubergine. (3) HIGHLIGHTS: painted-in manually, NOT following physical light math exactly — highlight placement emphasizes the emotional reading of the form (the cheekbone lit even when key is behind the subject, the knuckle catching a gleam on the swing). (4) SURFACE SPECIFICITY: skin has painted-in blush and pore mark visible at close-up, not subsurface scattering. Hair reads as sculpted painted volume — individual strands are grouped into painted clumps, each clump receiving a single highlight stroke. Fabric shows woven texture painted, not cloth-sim. (5) ENVIRONMENT: chunky mid-poly architecture with surface painting of grime strata, rust oxidation pattern, and iridescent oil-slick streaks on wet stone — a deep-industrial undercity aesthetic of chemical-district grime, never any named franchise district. Every metal surface carries thin-film interference rainbow spec. Background compresses into painterly matte with visible brushwork — never crisp photographic detail, never clean CGI surface. (6) POST: Vision3 500T film stock emulation — warm-highlight / cool-shadow curve, halation blooming on any pixel above 90% luminance (light sources bloom into surrounding haze), subtle chromatic aberration at high-contrast silhouette edges, 35mm-equivalent grain floor present but refined. IMPERATIVE: FORTICHE PAINTERLY-3D HYBRID. 3D forms with hand-painted surface shading, 2D-animated FX layer on top. FORBID: flat cel shading, clean untextured CGI, photoreal live-action light math, standard anime eye geometry, pure black. Line grammar: No hard cel outline anywhere. Silhouette carries entirely through painted edge — the brush stroke itself IS the boundary, weight varies from 2px at low emotional intensity to 5px at high, direction follows the form. Interior 'lines' are painted shadow steps: a steep value jump that reads as line at distance but resolves to brushwork at close. Shadow-side rim is painted deep violet, not inked. Effects layer (energy, fire, sparks) has its own distinct loose calligraphic brush contour that contrasts with the controlled character silhouette. Lens grammar: 35mm to 50mm portrait range. f/2.8 character close-up, f/4 mid-shot, f/5.6 wide establisher. Vision3 500T characteristic curve: warm highlight (+15 orange shift above 70% luminance), cool shadow (cyan push below 30%), halation blooming on anything above 90% luminance. 35mm grain floor present and refined. Mild chromatic aberration (1-2px lateral shift at silhouette). No anamorphic streak. 1.78:1 or 2.00:1 aspect. Light law: Single motivated key direction — warm industrial lantern, cool exterior moon, neon-adjacent accent from a painted window. Bounce fill is in the key's complementary hue from the palette: magenta key bounces teal fill, teal key bounces magenta fill. Rim accent color is always from the palette's jewel-tone (never white, never warm neutral). Ambient occlusion is painted soft and warm-dark, never a hard black AO contact shadow. Halation blooms on all practical sources in frame. Painted indirect-light from windows and lamps bleeds warm halos onto proximate geometry. Scene brief (Claude yazar): "Arada sırada bu tavernaya gelip birasını yudumlar ya da tepedeki bilge ile konuşurdu."" [SOURCE — do not render as on-screen text; narration only]. [DIRECTOR TASK — authored by Claude, not image content, do not print into the frame: compose ONE concrete single-frame scene faithful to this world's grammar above; author the dominant element and its half-second motion seed from the source.] Staging: logical hierarchy: components spatially ranked by importance, reading line clear, negative space guides the eye; strict composition — centered or negative-space led, frame edges intentional. Mood law: warm and tender - gentle unhurried pacing, soft warm palette, intimate close staging, emotional music motif. Reference anchor (subordinate to the world rendering law above — it colours HOW the frame is made, it never overrides the world, the palette or the negative lock): Fortiche painterly-3D: oil-paint brush stroke at every silhouette, deep-violet shadow (no black), teal+magenta two-color bounce, Vision3 500T halation, smear frames painted not motion-blurred; Fortiche undercity value-tension: painted-3D grime and oil-slick iridescence on wet industrial stone, deep-violet shadow (no black), teal-magenta rim system, thin-film metal spec, painterly smear frames, value-charged hold; Fortiche bridge-scale: monumental ornate painted-3D span, figure small against vast structure, warm-stone-and-gilt ornament against cool atmospheric sky, deep-violet shadow, painterly depth, held awe-of-scale. Camera grammar (this world's framing law — it governs the vantage; the move below happens inside it): 35-50mm portrait staging with a painted 3D weight: the character close-up carved out of a deep, softly falling background. The camera commits to one bold, deliberate move — a slow push onto the decisive gesture, a lateral track through the layers — then holds while the paint settles. Frame at the character's own height; the world is dense around them, never above them. Never a handheld shake, never a whip pan, never a top-down vantage. Camera/vantage: locked low-pressure angle held inside the frame, negative space carrying the wait. Composition pattern: rising diagonal — the dominant element enters on the lower-left third, its action line pointing toward upper-right light. Camera energy: slow location dolly or slider - reveal real space, practical depth and human-scale anchors before the hero beat. POV rule: witness POV — observe a real person or place without forcing performance; credibility over spectacle — only where it reveals the idea; a locked frame is valid. Light: hard value separation: one strong key, deep readable shadow shapes; warm motivated key with a named source (window, lamp, low sun). Light variant: motivate the key from the opposite side and let the shadow mass lead the composition. Time-of-day mandate: motivated window naturalism - believable direction, skin-safe falloff, room air and honest contrast. Palette physics: Palette light: near-black cool indigo, bright cool teal, vivid warm red, near-white warm ivory — palette character: deep-violet shadow + jewel-teal mid + magenta-pink accent + warm-cream highlight. Thin-film rainbow spec on all metal. Grime palette: rust-orange, oxidized copper, oil-slick iridescent (context-dependent). Render these as light behaviour, never flat fills. Texture rule: exactly ONE texture clause per prompt, from the "painterly" family — texture is seasoning, never the subject. Director mandate: Phase 0 preset: Sinematik Hikâye. Director thesis: Duyguyu reklam parlatmasıyla değil, gerçek mekan ve sinema grameriyle taşı. Text/logo: clean plate — this scene carries no on-screen text. No floating text, no caption, no subtitle, no watermark, no added signage; the image alone carries the meaning and the narration speaks it. Any Turkish text or logo already native to the scene is frozen geometry — only light and camera may cross it. Path contract (outranks the world grammar above when they disagree): the frame MUST deliver iP-safe style grammar: texture, light, value, camera, staging. Negative: copied characters, logos, exact shots; NO shimmer substance (iridescent purple glow serum); NO pure black anywhere in the frame — deepest shadow is deep violet; NO hard cel outline, NO standard anime flat-color shading; NO earth green, NO pure orange (flame accent excepted), NO sepia-brown palette; NOT anime; NO English signage; no recognizable franchise or real-person characters, logos, brand names; the named thing replaced by a symbol for it — no icon, pictogram, chart, diagram, arrow, gauge or infographic panel standing in for the real object or the real action; the literal thing is IN the frame, photographed/rendered as matter, not illustrated as a concept; empty adjectives (cinematic, dynamic, stunning, 4K); flat slide; warped or drifting text; character retyping. Clean motion-ready start frame.

Signature light: motivated single-direction key; bounce fill in complementary hue (if key is teal, fill is magenta-pink; if key is warm amber, fill is cool violet); rim light is always an accent color, NEVER white; hard-black ambient occlusion forbidden — AOcclusion is painterly-soft in shadow-hue, not black. Color/grade: Fortiche Vision3 500T-derived curve — warm-highlight, cool-shadow, painterly halation on hot pixels; deep-violet for the darkest shadow value (NO pure black anywhere); magenta-pink + teal two-color system in the painted shadows and rims. Lens/optics: 35–50mm, f/2.8–f/4; Vision3 500T halation on specular and emissive sources; 35mm-equivalent grain present in every value step, not in the highlights only. Texture/render: visible brush stroke at every silhouette edge; skin has hand-painted blush and pore work, NOT subsurface-scattering smooth; hair masses show individual paint-stroke separation, not smooth spec; highlights are painted-in decisions, not PBR physical math. Composition+motion: deliberate 24fps with hand-animated painted smear frames at peak action (2–3 frames, motion-streak painted); 1.5–2s emotional hold; slow-in slow-out with weight; no motion-blur.

Lens/optics: 35-50mm, f/2.8-f/4, halation on emissive sources, refined 35mm grain in every value step, 1-2px chromatic aberration at high-contrast silhouettes. prompts.image:
[15] IMAGE (motion start frame)
STYLE SYSTEM (the world rendering law — this is HOW to render, not WHAT; apply every clause below to the single concrete frame briefed next): Fortiche painterly-3D hybrid rendering as developed by Fortiche Production for premium episodic animation. This is a precise formal system, not 'anime-ish' — the grammar is industrial European painterly, not Japanese: (1) FORMS: full 3D geometry, but surface shading is a painted oil-texture layer — brush strokes are visible at every silhouette edge, their direction following the form's curvature but inconsistently, as if a painter made deliberate choices rather than a renderer solving physics. (2) COLOUR LAW: there is no black anywhere in this world. The deepest shadow is a rich deep violet. Skin shadow is rosewood-plum, architectural shadow is indigo-violet, cloth shadow is aubergine. (3) HIGHLIGHTS: painted-in manually, NOT following physical light math exactly — highlight placement emphasizes the emotional reading of the form (the cheekbone lit even when key is behind the subject, the knuckle catching a gleam on the swing). (4) SURFACE SPECIFICITY: skin has painted-in blush and pore mark visible at close-up, not subsurface scattering. Hair reads as sculpted painted volume — individual strands are grouped into painted clumps, each clump receiving a single highlight stroke. Fabric shows woven texture painted, not cloth-sim. (5) ENVIRONMENT: chunky mid-poly architecture with surface painting of grime strata, rust oxidation pattern, and iridescent oil-slick streaks on wet stone — a deep-industrial undercity aesthetic of chemical-district grime, never any named franchise district. Every metal surface carries thin-film interference rainbow spec. Background compresses into painterly matte with visible brushwork — never crisp photographic detail, never clean CGI surface. (6) POST: Vision3 500T film stock emulation — warm-highlight / cool-shadow curve, halation blooming on any pixel above 90% luminance (light sources bloom into surrounding haze), subtle chromatic aberration at high-contrast silhouette edges, 35mm-equivalent grain floor present but refined. IMPERATIVE: FORTICHE PAINTERLY-3D HYBRID. 3D forms with hand-painted surface shading, 2D-animated FX layer on top. FORBID: flat cel shading, clean untextured CGI, photoreal live-action light math, standard anime eye geometry, pure black. Line grammar: No hard cel outline anywhere. Silhouette carries entirely through painted edge — the brush stroke itself IS the boundary, weight varies from 2px at low emotional intensity to 5px at high, direction follows the form. Interior 'lines' are painted shadow steps: a steep value jump that reads as line at distance but resolves to brushwork at close. Shadow-side rim is painted deep violet, not inked. Effects layer (energy, fire, sparks) has its own distinct loose calligraphic brush contour that contrasts with the controlled character silhouette. Lens grammar: 35mm to 50mm portrait range. f/2.8 character close-up, f/4 mid-shot, f/5.6 wide establisher. Vision3 500T characteristic curve: warm highlight (+15 orange shift above 70% luminance), cool shadow (cyan push below 30%), halation blooming on anything above 90% luminance. 35mm grain floor present and refined. Mild chromatic aberration (1-2px lateral shift at silhouette). No anamorphic streak. 1.78:1 or 2.00:1 aspect. Light law: Single motivated key direction — warm industrial lantern, cool exterior moon, neon-adjacent accent from a painted window. Bounce fill is in the key's complementary hue from the palette: magenta key bounces teal fill, teal key bounces magenta fill. Rim accent color is always from the palette's jewel-tone (never white, never warm neutral). Ambient occlusion is painted soft and warm-dark, never a hard black AO contact shadow. Halation blooms on all practical sources in frame. Painted indirect-light from windows and lamps bleeds warm halos onto proximate geometry. Scene brief (Claude yazar): ""Son kez suya açıldığından beri kimse onu bir daha görmedi." [SOURCE — do not render as on-screen text; narration only]. [DIRECTOR TASK — authored by Claude, not image content, do not print into the frame: compose ONE concrete single-frame scene faithful to this world's grammar above; author the dominant element and its half-second motion seed from the source.] Staging: logical hierarchy: components spatially ranked by importance, reading line clear, negative space guides the eye; strict composition — centered or negative-space led, frame edges intentional. Mood law: warm and tender - gentle unhurried pacing, soft warm palette, intimate close staging, emotional music motif. Reference anchor (subordinate to the world rendering law above — it colours HOW the frame is made, it never overrides the world, the palette or the negative lock): Fortiche painterly-3D: oil-paint brush stroke at every silhouette, deep-violet shadow (no black), teal+magenta two-color bounce, Vision3 500T halation, smear frames painted not motion-blurred; Fortiche undercity value-tension: painted-3D grime and oil-slick iridescence on wet industrial stone, deep-violet shadow (no black), teal-magenta rim system, thin-film metal spec, painterly smear frames, value-charged hold; Fortiche bridge-scale: monumental ornate painted-3D span, figure small against vast structure, warm-stone-and-gilt ornament against cool atmospheric sky, deep-violet shadow, painterly depth, held awe-of-scale. Camera grammar (this world's framing law — it governs the vantage; the move below happens inside it): 35-50mm portrait staging with a painted 3D weight: the character close-up carved out of a deep, softly falling background. The camera commits to one bold, deliberate move — a slow push onto the decisive gesture, a lateral track through the layers — then holds while the paint settles. Frame at the character's own height; the world is dense around them, never above them. Never a handheld shake, never a whip pan, never a top-down vantage. Camera/vantage: slow push along the dominant silhouette edge, value planes parallaxing behind. Composition pattern: negative-space dominance (ma) — the dominant element occupies roughly one quarter of frame, disciplined emptiness carrying the rest. Camera energy: slow location dolly or slider - reveal real space, practical depth and human-scale anchors before the hero beat. POV rule: witness POV — observe a real person or place without forcing performance; credibility over spectacle — only where it reveals the idea; a locked frame is valid. Light: hard value separation: one strong key, deep readable shadow shapes; warm motivated key with a named source (window, lamp, low sun). Time-of-day mandate: motivated window naturalism - believable direction, skin-safe falloff, room air and honest contrast. Palette physics: Palette light: near-black cool indigo, bright cool teal, vivid warm red, near-white warm ivory — palette character: deep-violet shadow + jewel-teal mid + magenta-pink accent + warm-cream highlight. Thin-film rainbow spec on all metal. Grime palette: rust-orange, oxidized copper, oil-slick iridescent (context-dependent). Render these as light behaviour, never flat fills. Texture rule: exactly ONE texture clause per prompt, from the "painterly" family — texture is seasoning, never the subject. Director mandate: Phase 0 preset: Sinematik Hikâye. Director thesis: Duyguyu reklam parlatmasıyla değil, gerçek mekan ve sinema grameriyle taşı. Text/logo: clean plate — this scene carries no on-screen text. No floating text, no caption, no subtitle, no watermark, no added signage; the image alone carries the meaning and the narration speaks it. Any Turkish text or logo already native to the scene is frozen geometry — only light and camera may cross it. Path contract (outranks the world grammar above when they disagree): the frame MUST deliver iP-safe style grammar: texture, light, value, camera, staging. Negative: copied characters, logos, exact shots; NO shimmer substance (iridescent purple glow serum); NO pure black anywhere in the frame — deepest shadow is deep violet; NO hard cel outline, NO standard anime flat-color shading; NO earth green, NO pure orange (flame accent excepted), NO sepia-brown palette; NOT anime; NO English signage; no recognizable franchise or real-person characters, logos, brand names; the named thing replaced by a symbol for it — no icon, pictogram, chart, diagram, arrow, gauge or infographic panel standing in for the real object or the real action; the literal thing is IN the frame, photographed/rendered as matter, not illustrated as a concept; empty adjectives (cinematic, dynamic, stunning, 4K); flat slide; warped or drifting text; character retyping. Clean motion-ready start frame.

Motion cadence (this world's own motion signature — the authority for HOW this world moves; obey its cadence discipline exactly, but where it names a hold duration the engine law's final-hold timing above supersedes): Deliberate 24fps with hand-animated painted smear frames at peak action — 2-3 consecutive frames with painted directional motion streak visible as brushwork, not motion-blur. 1.5-2 second hold on emotional beat — character studies the situation, eyes tracking. Slow-in / slow-out weight. Momentum carries: a punch's aftershock ripples through cloth and hair for 4-6 frames after impact. No motion blur — smears are painted. Engine grammar (Kling 3.0): Kling 3.0 start-frame fidelity: the approved frame is truth — describe only what changes, never re-describe what already exists; the motion reads as the frame's next half-second. Everything not named stays exactly as the start frame shows: world, material, light, faces, text, logo, geometry — never re-described, never re-rendered.
NEGATIVE: morphing, warping, re-render, style or material drift, new objects or scenery, leaving the frame, face or identity change, mouth movement, logo/text/geometry change, multiple actions, flicker, re-render of the start frame.
[DIRECTOR TASK — frame-specific negatives, do not print into the clip: before finalizing, specialize the baseline negative above to THIS approved frame — name its actual fragile elements (the exact text plate, thin rigging/lines, a reflection edge, the logo) in place of the generic list.]

POV rule: witness POV — observe a real person or place without forcing performance; credibility over spectacle — only where it reveals the idea; a locked frame is valid. Light: hard value separation: one strong key, deep readable shadow shapes; warm motivated key with a named source (window, lamp, low sun). Time-of-day mandate: motivated window naturalism - believable direction, skin-safe falloff, room air and honest contrast. Palette physics: Palette light: near-black cool indigo, bright cool teal, vivid warm red, near-white warm ivory — palette character: deep-violet shadow + jewel-teal mid + magenta-pink accent + warm-cream highlight. Thin-film rainbow spec on all metal. Grime palette: rust-orange, oxidized copper, oil-slick iridescent (context-dependent). Render these as light behaviour, never flat fills. Texture rule: exactly ONE texture clause per prompt, from the "painterly" family — texture is seasoning, never the subject. Director mandate: Phase 0 preset: Sinematik Hikâye. Director thesis: Duyguyu reklam parlatmasıyla değil, gerçek mekan ve sinema grameriyle taşı. Text/logo: clean plate — this scene carries no on-screen text. No floating text, no caption, no subtitle, no watermark, no added signage; the image alone carries the meaning and the narration speaks it. Any Turkish text or logo already native to the scene is frozen geometry — only light and camera may cross it. Path contract (outranks the world grammar above when they disagree): the frame MUST deliver iP-safe style grammar: texture, light, value, camera, staging. Negative: copied characters, logos, exact shots; NO shimmer substance (iridescent purple glow serum); NO pure black anywhere in the frame — deepest shadow is deep violet; NO hard cel outline, NO standard anime flat-color shading; NO earth green, NO pure orange (flame accent excepted), NO sepia-brown palette; NOT anime; NO English signage; no recognizable franchise or real-person characters, logos, brand names; the named thing replaced by a symbol for it — no icon, pictogram, chart, diagram, arrow, gauge or infographic panel standing in for the real object or the real action; the literal thing is IN the frame, photographed/rendered as matter, not illustrated as a concept; empty adjectives (cinematic, dynamic, stunning, 4K); flat slide; warped or drifting text; character retyping. Clean motion-ready start frame.

world.renderRecipe: Fortiche painterly-3D hybrid rendering as developed by Fortiche Production for premium episodic animation. This is a precise formal system, not 'anime-ish' — the grammar is industrial European painterly, not Japanese: (1) FORMS: full 3D geometry, but surface shading is a painted oil-texture layer — brush strokes are visible at every silhouette edge, their direction following the form's curvature but inconsistently, as if a painter made deliberate choices rather than a renderer solving physics. (2) COLOUR LAW: there is no black anywhere in this world. The deepest shadow is a rich deep violet. Skin shadow is rosewood-plum, architectural shadow is indigo-violet, cloth shadow is aubergine. (3) HIGHLIGHTS: painted-in manually, NOT following physical light math exactly — highlight placement emphasizes the emotional reading of the form (the cheekbone lit even when key is behind the subject, the knuckle catching a gleam on the swing). (4) SURFACE SPECIFICITY: skin has painted-in blush and pore mark visible at close-up, not subsurface scattering. Hair reads as sculpted painted volume — individual strands are grouped into painted clumps, each clump receiving a single highlight stroke. Fabric shows woven texture painted, not cloth-sim. (5) ENVIRONMENT: chunky mid-poly architecture with surface painting of grime strata, rust oxidation pattern, and iridescent oil-slick streaks on wet stone — a deep-industrial undercity aesthetic of chemical-district grime, never any named franchise district. Every metal surface carries thin-film interference rainbow spec. Background compresses into painterly matte with visible brushwork — never crisp photographic detail, never clean CGI surface. (6) POST: Vision3 500T film stock emulation — warm-highlight / cool-shadow curve, halation blooming on any pixel above 90% luminance (light sources bloom into surrounding haze), subtle chromatic aberration at high-contrast silhouette edges, 35mm-equivalent grain floor present but refined. IMPERATIVE: FORTICHE PAINTERLY-3D HYBRID. 3D forms with hand-painted surface shading, 2D-animated FX layer on top. FORBID: flat cel shading, clean untextured CGI, photoreal live-action light math, standard anime eye geometry, pure black.
world.camera: 50mm high three-quarter medium view, cause-and-effect layout visible in one frame
world.motionGrammar: Deliberate 24fps with hand-animated painted smear frames at peak action — 2-3 consecutive frames with painted directional motion streak visible as brushwork, not motion-blur. 1.5-2 second hold on emotional beat — character studies the situation, eyes tracking. Slow-in / slow-out weight. Momentum carries: a punch's aftershock ripples through cloth and hair for 4-6 frames after impact. No motion blur — smears are painted.

refDNAs.name: Fortiche Painted Texture Grammar

Named anchor: Fortiche impasto 2D-over-3D surface treatment — NOT anime, NOT cel — paint stroke IS the shading method. Signature light: motivated single-direction key; bounce fill in complementary hue (if key is teal, fill is magenta-pink; if key is warm amber, fill is cool violet); rim light is always an accent color, NEVER white; hard-black ambient occlusion forbidden — AOcclusion is painterly-soft in shadow-hue, not black. Lens/optics: 35–50mm, f/2.8–f/4; Vision3 500T halation on specular and emissive sources; 35mm-equivalent grain present in every value step, not in the highlights only. Texture/render: visible brush stroke at every silhouette edge; skin has hand-painted blush and pore work, NOT subsurface-scattering smooth; hair masses show individual paint-stroke separation, not smooth spec; highlights are painted-in decisions, not PBR physical math. Composition+motion: deliberate 24fps with hand-animated painted smear frames at peak action (2–3 frames, motion-streak painted); 1.5–2s emotional hold; slow-in slow-out with weight; no motion-blur.

Interior 'lines' are painted shadow steps: a steep value jump that reads as line at distance but resolves to brushwork at close. Shadow-side rim is painted deep violet, not inked. Effects layer (energy, fire, sparks) has its own distinct loose calligraphic brush contour that contrasts with the controlled character silhouette. Lens grammar: 35mm to 50mm portrait range. f/2.8 character close-up, f/4 mid-shot, f/5.6 wide establisher. Vision3 500T characteristic curve: warm highlight (+15 orange shift above 70% luminance), cool shadow (cyan push below 30%), halation blooming on anything above 90% luminance. 35mm grain floor present and refined. Mild chromatic aberration (1-2px lateral shift at silhouette). No anamorphic streak. 1.78:1 or 2.00:1 aspect. Light law: Single motivated key direction — warm industrial lantern, cool exterior moon, neon-adjacent accent from a painted window. Bounce fill is in the key's complementary hue from the palette: magenta key bounces teal fill, teal key bounces magenta fill. Rim accent color is always from the palette's jewel-tone (never white, never warm neutral). Ambient occlusion is painted soft and warm-dark, never a hard black AO contact shadow. Halation blooms on all practical sources in frame. Painted indirect-light from windows and lamps bleeds warm halos onto proximate geometry. Mood law: warm and tender - gentle unhurried pacing, soft warm palette, intimate close staging, emotional music motif. The camera commits to one bold, deliberate move — a slow push onto the decisive gesture, a lateral track through the layers — then holds while the paint settles. Frame at the character's own height; the world is dense around them, never above them. Never a handheld shake, never a whip pan, never a top-down vantage. Composition pattern: frame within frame — an aperture already in the scene (window, shelf gap, arch, doorway) contains the dominant element, its border one value darker than what it holds. Camera energy: slow location dolly or slider - reveal real space, practical depth and human-scale anchors before the hero beat. world.renderRecipe: Fortiche painterly-3D hybrid rendering as developed by Fortiche Production for premium episodic animation. This is a precise formal system, not 'anime-ish' — the grammar is industrial European painterly, not Japanese: (1) FORMS: full 3D geometry, but surface shading is a painted oil-texture layer — brush strokes are visible at every silhouette edge, their direction following the form's curvature but inconsistently, as if a painter made deliberate choices rather than a renderer solving physics. (2) COLOUR LAW: there is no black anywhere in this world. The deepest shadow is a rich deep violet. Skin shadow is rosewood-plum, architectural shadow is indigo-violet, cloth shadow is aubergine. (3) HIGHLIGHTS: painted-in manually, NOT following physical light math exactly — highlight placement emphasizes the emotional reading of the form (the cheekbone lit even when key is behind the subject, the knuckle catching a gleam on the swing). (4) SURFACE SPECIFICITY: skin has painted-in blush and pore mark visible at close-up, not subsurface scattering. Hair reads as sculpted painted volume — individual strands are grouped into painted clumps, each clump receiving a single highlight stroke. Fabric shows woven texture painted, not cloth-sim. (5) ENVIRONMENT: chunky mid-poly architecture with surface painting of grime strata, rust oxidation pattern, and iridescent oil-slick streaks on wet stone — a deep-industrial undercity aesthetic of chemical-district grime, never any named franchise district. Every metal surface carries thin-film interference rainbow spec. Background compresses into painterly matte with visible brushwork — never crisp photographic detail, never clean CGI surface. (6) POST: Vision3 500T film stock emulation — warm-highlight / cool-shadow curve, halation blooming on any pixel above 90% luminance (light sources bloom into surrounding haze), subtle chromatic aberration at high-contrast silhouette edges, 35mm-equivalent grain floor present but refined. IMPERATIVE: FORTICHE PAINTERLY-3D HYBRID. 3D forms with hand-painted surface shading, 2D-animated FX layer on top. FORBID: flat cel shading, clean untextured CGI, photoreal live-action light math, standard anime eye geometry, pure black.

Slow-in / slow-out weight. Momentum carries: a punch's aftershock ripples through cloth and hair for 4-6 frames after impact. No motion blur — smears are painted.

world.renderRecipe: Fortiche painterly-3D hybrid rendering as developed by Fortiche Production for premium episodic animation. This is a precise formal system, not 'anime-ish' — the grammar is industrial European painterly, not Japanese: (1) FORMS: full 3D geometry, but surface shading is a painted oil-texture layer — brush strokes are visible at every silhouette edge, their direction following the form's curvature but inconsistently, as if a painter made deliberate choices rather than a renderer solving physics. (2) COLOUR LAW: there is no black anywhere in this world. The deepest shadow is a rich deep violet. Skin shadow is rosewood-plum, architectural shadow is indigo-violet, cloth shadow is aubergine. (3) HIGHLIGHTS: painted-in manually, NOT following physical light math exactly — highlight placement emphasizes the emotional reading of the form (the cheekbone lit even when key is behind the subject, the knuckle catching a gleam on the swing). (4) SURFACE SPECIFICITY: skin has painted-in blush and pore mark visible at close-up, not subsurface scattering. Hair reads as sculpted painted volume — individual strands are grouped into painted clumps, each clump receiving a single highlight stroke. Fabric shows woven texture painted, not cloth-sim. (5) ENVIRONMENT: chunky mid-poly architecture with surface painting of grime strata, rust oxidation pattern, and iridescent oil-slick streaks on wet stone — a deep-industrial undercity aesthetic of chemical-district grime, never any named franchise district. Every metal surface carries thin-film interference rainbow spec. Background compresses into painterly matte with visible brushwork — never crisp photographic detail, never clean CGI surface. (6) POST: Vision3 500T film stock emulation — warm-highlight / cool-shadow curve, halation blooming on any pixel above 90% luminance (light sources bloom into surrounding haze), subtle chromatic aberration at high-contrast silhouette edges, 35mm-equivalent grain floor present but refined. IMPERATIVE: FORTICHE PAINTERLY-3D HYBRID. 3D forms with hand-painted surface shading, 2D-animated FX layer on top. FORBID: flat cel shading, clean untextured CGI, photoreal live-action light math, standard anime eye geometry, pure black.

Slow-in / slow-out weight. Momentum carries: a punch's aftershock ripples through cloth and hair for 4-6 frames after impact. No motion blur — smears are painted.

refDNAs.name: Fortiche Painted Texture Grammar
refDNAs.directive: Stylized animation frame in the Fortiche Production painterly-3D doctrine ( season 1 and 2). Medium/era: 2021–2024 Fortiche hybrid — 3D-modeled forms surface-shaded with oil-paint texture hand-applied at every silhouette edge. Named anchor: Fortiche impasto 2D-over-3D surface treatment — NOT anime, NOT cel — paint stroke IS the shading method. Signature light: motivated single-direction key; bounce fill in complementary hue (if key is teal, fill is magenta-pink; if key is warm amber, fill is cool violet); rim light is always an accent color, NEVER white; hard-black ambient occlusion forbidden — AOcclusion is painterly-soft in shadow-hue, not black. Color/grade: Fortiche Vision3 500T-derived curve — warm-highlight, cool-shadow, painterly halation on hot pixels; deep-violet for the darkest shadow value (NO pure black anywhere); magenta-pink + teal two-color system in the painted shadows and rims. Lens/optics: 35–50mm, f/2.8–f/4; Vision3 500T halation on specular and emissive sources; 35mm-equivalent grain present in every value step, not in the highlights only. Texture/render: visible brush stroke at every silhouette edge; skin has hand-painted blush and pore work, NOT subsurface-scattering smooth; hair masses show individual paint-stroke separation, not smooth spec; highlights are painted-in decisions, not PBR physical math. Composition+motion: deliberate 24fps with hand-animated painted smear frames at peak action (2–3 frames, motion-streak painted); 1.5–2s emotional hold; slow-in slow-out with weight; no motion-blur.

(3) HIGHLIGHTS: painted-in manually, NOT following physical light math exactly — highlight placement emphasizes the emotional reading of the form (the cheekbone lit even when key is behind the subject, the knuckle catching a gleam on the swing). (4) SURFACE SPECIFICITY: skin has painted-in blush and pore mark visible at close-up, not subsurface scattering. Hair reads as sculpted painted volume — individual strands are grouped into painted clumps, each clump receiving a single highlight stroke. Fabric shows woven texture painted, not cloth-sim. (5) ENVIRONMENT: chunky mid-poly architecture with surface painting of grime strata, rust oxidation pattern, and iridescent oil-slick streaks on wet stone — a deep-industrial undercity aesthetic of chemical-district grime, never any named franchise district. Every metal surface carries thin-film interference rainbow spec. Background compresses into painterly matte with visible brushwork — never crisp photographic detail, never clean CGI surface. (6) POST: Vision3 500T film stock emulation — warm-highlight / cool-shadow curve, halation blooming on any pixel above 90% luminance (light sources bloom into surrounding haze), subtle chromatic aberration at high-contrast silhouette edges, 35mm-equivalent grain floor present but refined. IMPERATIVE: FORTICHE PAINTERLY-3D HYBRID. 3D forms with hand-painted surface shading, 2D-animated FX layer on top. FORBID: flat cel shading, clean untextured CGI, photoreal live-action light math, standard anime eye geometry, pure black. Line grammar: No hard cel outline anywhere. Silhouette carries entirely through painted edge — the brush stroke itself IS the boundary, weight varies from 2px at low emotional intensity to 5px at high, direction follows the form. Interior 'lines' are painted shadow steps: a steep value jump that reads as line at distance but resolves to brushwork at close. Shadow-side rim is painted deep violet, not inked. Effects layer (energy, fire, sparks) has its own distinct loose calligraphic brush contour that contrasts with the controlled character silhouette. Lens grammar: 35mm to 50mm portrait range. f/2.8 character close-up, f/4 mid-shot, f/5.6 wide establisher. Vision3 500T characteristic curve: warm highlight (+15 orange shift above 70% luminance), cool shadow (cyan push below 30%), halation blooming on anything above 90% luminance. 35mm grain floor present and refined. Mild chromatic aberration (1-2px lateral shift at silhouette). No anamorphic streak. 1.78:1 or 2.00:1 aspect. Light law: Single motivated key direction — warm industrial lantern, cool exterior moon, neon-adjacent accent from a painted window. Bounce fill is in the key's complementary hue from the palette: magenta key bounces teal fill, teal key bounces magenta fill. Rim accent color is always from the palette's jewel-tone (never white, never warm neutral). Ambient occlusion is painted soft and warm-dark, never a hard black AO contact shadow. Halation blooms on all practical sources in frame. Painted indirect-light from windows and lamps bleeds warm halos onto proximate geometry. Scene brief (Claude yazar): "Ancak teknesi Jennessee'nin geçtiğimiz günlerde limana kendi kendine döndüğünü görenler olmuş." [SOURCE — do not render as on-screen text; narration only]. [DIRECTOR TASK — authored by Claude, not image content, do not print into the frame: compose ONE concrete single-frame scene faithful to this world's grammar above; author the dominant element and its half-second motion seed from the source.] Staging: logical hierarchy: components spatially ranked by importance, reading line clear, negative space guides the eye; strict composition — centered or negative-space led, frame edges intentional. Mood law: warm and tender - gentle unhurried pacing, soft warm palette, intimate close staging, emotional music motif. Reference anchor (subordinate to the world rendering law above — it colours HOW the frame is made, it never overrides the world, the palette or the negative lock): Fortiche painterly-3D: oil-paint brush stroke at every silhouette, deep-violet shadow (no black), teal+magenta two-color bounce, Vision3 500T halation, smear frames painted not motion-blurred; Fortiche undercity value-tension: painted-3D grime and oil-slick iridescence on wet industrial stone, deep-violet shadow (no black), teal-magenta rim system, thin-film metal spec, painterly smear frames, value-charged hold; Fortiche bridge-scale: monumental ornate painted-3D span, figure small against vast structure, warm-stone-and-gilt ornament against cool atmospheric sky, deep-violet shadow, painterly depth, held awe-of-scale. Camera grammar (this world's framing law — it governs the vantage; the move below happens inside it): 35-50mm portrait staging with a painted 3D weight: the character close-up carved out of a deep, softly falling background. The camera commits to one bold, deliberate move — a slow push onto the decisive gesture, a lateral track through the layers — then holds while the paint settles. Frame at the character's own height; the world is dense around them, never above them. Never a handheld shake, never a whip pan, never a top-down vantage. Camera/vantage: slow push along the dominant silhouette edge, value planes parallaxing behind. Composition pattern: rising diagonal — the dominant element enters on the lower-left third, its action line pointing toward upper-right light. Camera energy: slow location dolly or slider - reveal real space, practical depth and human-scale anchors before the hero beat. POV rule: witness POV — observe a real person or place without forcing performance; credibility over spectacle — only where it reveals the idea; a locked frame is valid. Light: hard value separation: one strong key, deep readable shadow shapes; warm motivated key with a named source (window, lamp, low sun). Light variant: motivate the key from the opposite side and let the shadow mass lead the composition. Time-of-day mandate: motivated window naturalism - believable direction, skin-safe falloff, room air and honest contrast. Palette physics: Palette light: near-black cool indigo, bright cool teal, vivid warm red, near-white warm ivory — palette character: deep-violet shadow + jewel-teal mid + magenta-pink accent + warm-cream highlight. Thin-film rainbow spec on all metal. Grime palette: rust-orange, oxidized copper, oil-slick iridescent (context-dependent). Render these as light behaviour, never flat fills. Texture rule: exactly ONE texture clause per prompt, from the "painterly" family — texture is seasoning, never the subject. Director mandate: Phase 0 preset: Sinematik Hikâye. Director thesis: Duyguyu reklam parlatmasıyla değil, gerçek mekan ve sinema grameriyle taşı. Text/logo: clean plate — this scene carries no on-screen text. No floating text, no caption, no subtitle, no watermark, no added signage; the image alone carries the meaning and the narration speaks it. Any Turkish text or logo already native to the scene is frozen geometry — only light and camera may cross it. Path contract (outranks the world grammar above when they disagree): the frame MUST deliver iP-safe style grammar: texture, light, value, camera, staging. Negative: copied characters, logos, exact shots; NO shimmer substance (iridescent purple glow serum); NO pure black anywhere in the frame — deepest shadow is deep violet; NO hard cel outline, NO standard anime flat-color shading; NO earth green, NO pure orange (flame accent excepted), NO sepia-brown palette; NOT anime; NO English signage; no recognizable franchise or real-person characters, logos, brand names; the named thing replaced by a symbol for it — no icon, pictogram, chart, diagram, arrow, gauge or infographic panel standing in for the real object or the real action; the literal thing is IN the frame, photographed/rendered as matter, not illustrated as a concept; empty adjectives (cinematic, dynamic, stunning, 4K); flat slide; warped or drifting text; character retyping. This is a precise formal system, not 'anime-ish' — the grammar is industrial European painterly, not Japanese: (1) FORMS: full 3D geometry, but surface shading is a painted oil-texture layer — brush strokes are visible at every silhouette edge, their direction following the form's curvature but inconsistently, as if a painter made deliberate choices rather than a renderer solving physics. (2) COLOUR LAW: there is no black anywhere in this world. The deepest shadow is a rich deep violet. Skin shadow is rosewood-plum, architectural shadow is indigo-violet, cloth shadow is aubergine. (3) HIGHLIGHTS: painted-in manually, NOT following physical light math exactly — highlight placement emphasizes the emotional reading of the form (the cheekbone lit even when key is behind the subject, the knuckle catching a gleam on the swing). (4) SURFACE SPECIFICITY: skin has painted-in blush and pore mark visible at close-up, not subsurface scattering. Hair reads as sculpted painted volume — individual strands are grouped into painted clumps, each clump receiving a single highlight stroke. Fabric shows woven texture painted, not cloth-sim. (5) ENVIRONMENT: chunky mid-poly architecture with surface painting of grime strata, rust oxidation pattern, and iridescent oil-slick streaks on wet stone — a deep-industrial undercity aesthetic of chemical-district grime, never any named franchise district. Every metal surface carries thin-film interference rainbow spec. Background compresses into painterly matte with visible brushwork — never crisp photographic detail, never clean CGI surface. (6) POST: Vision3 500T film stock emulation — warm-highlight / cool-shadow curve, halation blooming on any pixel above 90% luminance (light sources bloom into surrounding haze), subtle chromatic aberration at high-contrast silhouette edges, 35mm-equivalent grain floor present but refined. IMPERATIVE: FORTICHE PAINTERLY-3D HYBRID. 3D forms with hand-painted surface shading, 2D-animated FX layer on top. FORBID: flat cel shading, clean untextured CGI, photoreal live-action light math, standard anime eye geometry, pure black.

(3) HIGHLIGHTS: painted-in manually, NOT following physical light math exactly — highlight placement emphasizes the emotional reading of the form (the cheekbone lit even when key is behind the subject, the knuckle catching a gleam on the swing). (4) SURFACE SPECIFICITY: skin has painted-in blush and pore mark visible at close-up, not subsurface scattering. Hair reads as sculpted painted volume — individual strands are grouped into painted clumps, each clump receiving a single highlight stroke. Fabric shows woven texture painted, not cloth-sim. (5) ENVIRONMENT: chunky mid-poly architecture with surface painting of grime strata, rust oxidation pattern, and iridescent oil-slick streaks on wet stone — a deep-industrial undercity aesthetic of chemical-district grime, never any named franchise district. Every metal surface carries thin-film interference rainbow spec. Background compresses into painterly matte with visible brushwork — never crisp photographic detail, never clean CGI surface. (6) POST: Vision3 500T film stock emulation — warm-highlight / cool-shadow curve, halation blooming on any pixel above 90% luminance (light sources bloom into surrounding haze), subtle chromatic aberration at high-contrast silhouette edges, 35mm-equivalent grain floor present but refined. IMPERATIVE: FORTICHE PAINTERLY-3D HYBRID. 3D forms with hand-painted surface shading, 2D-animated FX layer on top. FORBID: flat cel shading, clean untextured CGI, photoreal live-action light math, standard anime eye geometry, pure black. Line grammar: No hard cel outline anywhere. Silhouette carries entirely through painted edge — the brush stroke itself IS the boundary, weight varies from 2px at low emotional intensity to 5px at high, direction follows the form. Interior 'lines' are painted shadow steps: a steep value jump that reads as line at distance but resolves to brushwork at close. Shadow-side rim is painted deep violet, not inked. Effects layer (energy, fire, sparks) has its own distinct loose calligraphic brush contour that contrasts with the controlled character silhouette. Lens grammar: 35mm to 50mm portrait range. f/2.8 character close-up, f/4 mid-shot, f/5.6 wide establisher. Vision3 500T characteristic curve: warm highlight (+15 orange shift above 70% luminance), cool shadow (cyan push below 30%), halation blooming on anything above 90% luminance. 35mm grain floor present and refined. Mild chromatic aberration (1-2px lateral shift at silhouette). No anamorphic streak. 1.78:1 or 2.00:1 aspect. Light law: Single motivated key direction — warm industrial lantern, cool exterior moon, neon-adjacent accent from a painted window. Bounce fill is in the key's complementary hue from the palette: magenta key bounces teal fill, teal key bounces magenta fill. Rim accent color is always from the palette's jewel-tone (never white, never warm neutral). Ambient occlusion is painted soft and warm-dark, never a hard black AO contact shadow. Halation blooms on all practical sources in frame. Painted indirect-light from windows and lamps bleeds warm halos onto proximate geometry. Scene brief (Claude yazar): "Ancak teknesi Jennessee'nin geçtiğimiz günlerde limana kendi kendine döndüğünü görenler olmuş." [SOURCE — do not render as on-screen text; narration only]. [DIRECTOR TASK — authored by Claude, not image content, do not print into the frame: compose ONE concrete single-frame scene faithful to this world's grammar above; author the dominant element and its half-second motion seed from the source.] Staging: logical hierarchy: components spatially ranked by importance, reading line clear, negative space guides the eye; strict composition — centered or negative-space led, frame edges intentional. Mood law: warm and tender - gentle unhurried pacing, soft warm palette, intimate close staging, emotional music motif. Reference anchor (subordinate to the world rendering law above — it colours HOW the frame is made, it never overrides the world, the palette or the negative lock): Fortiche painterly-3D: oil-paint brush stroke at every silhouette, deep-violet shadow (no black), teal+magenta two-color bounce, Vision3 500T halation, smear frames painted not motion-blurred; Fortiche undercity value-tension: painted-3D grime and oil-slick iridescence on wet industrial stone, deep-violet shadow (no black), teal-magenta rim system, thin-film metal spec, painterly smear frames, value-charged hold; Fortiche bridge-scale: monumental ornate painted-3D span, figure small against vast structure, warm-stone-and-gilt ornament against cool atmospheric sky, deep-violet shadow, painterly depth, held awe-of-scale. Camera grammar (this world's framing law — it governs the vantage; the move below happens inside it): 35-50mm portrait staging with a painted 3D weight: the character close-up carved out of a deep, softly falling background. The camera commits to one bold, deliberate move — a slow push onto the decisive gesture, a lateral track through the layers — then holds while the paint settles. Frame at the character's own height; the world is dense around them, never above them. Never a handheld shake, never a whip pan, never a top-down vantage. Camera/vantage: slow push along the dominant silhouette edge, value planes parallaxing behind. Composition pattern: rising diagonal — the dominant element enters on the lower-left third, its action line pointing toward upper-right light. Camera energy: slow location dolly or slider - reveal real space, practical depth and human-scale anchors before the hero beat. POV rule: witness POV — observe a real person or place without forcing performance; credibility over spectacle — only where it reveals the idea; a locked frame is valid. Light: hard value separation: one strong key, deep readable shadow shapes; warm motivated key with a named source (window, lamp, low sun). Light variant: motivate the key from the opposite side and let the shadow mass lead the composition. Time-of-day mandate: motivated window naturalism - believable direction, skin-safe falloff, room air and honest contrast. Palette physics: Palette light: near-black cool indigo, bright cool teal, vivid warm red, near-white warm ivory — palette character: deep-violet shadow + jewel-teal mid + magenta-pink accent + warm-cream highlight. Thin-film rainbow spec on all metal. Grime palette: rust-orange, oxidized copper, oil-slick iridescent (context-dependent). Render these as light behaviour, never flat fills. Texture rule: exactly ONE texture clause per prompt, from the "painterly" family — texture is seasoning, never the subject. Director mandate: Phase 0 preset: Sinematik Hikâye. Director thesis: Duyguyu reklam parlatmasıyla değil, gerçek mekan ve sinema grameriyle taşı. Text/logo: clean plate — this scene carries no on-screen text. No floating text, no caption, no subtitle, no watermark, no added signage; the image alone carries the meaning and the narration speaks it. Any Turkish text or logo already native to the scene is frozen geometry — only light and camera may cross it. Path contract (outranks the world grammar above when they disagree): the frame MUST deliver iP-safe style grammar: texture, light, value, camera, staging. Negative: copied characters, logos, exact shots; NO shimmer substance (iridescent purple glow serum); NO pure black anywhere in the frame — deepest shadow is deep violet; NO hard cel outline, NO standard anime flat-color shading; NO earth green, NO pure orange (flame accent excepted), NO sepia-brown palette; NOT anime; NO English signage; no recognizable franchise or real-person characters, logos, brand names; the named thing replaced by a symbol for it — no icon, pictogram, chart, diagram, arrow, gauge or infographic panel standing in for the real object or the real action; the literal thing is IN the frame, photographed/rendered as matter, not illustrated as a concept; empty adjectives (cinematic, dynamic, stunning, 4K); flat slide; warped or drifting text; character retyping. Clean motion-ready start frame.

world.renderRecipe: Fortiche painterly-3D hybrid rendering as developed by Fortiche Production for premium episodic animation. This is a precise formal system, not 'anime-ish' — the grammar is industrial European painterly, not Japanese: (1) FORMS: full 3D geometry, but surface shading is a painted oil-texture layer — brush strokes are visible at every silhouette edge, their direction following the form's curvature but inconsistently, as if a painter made deliberate choices rather than a renderer solving physics. (2) COLOUR LAW: there is no black anywhere in this world. The deepest shadow is a rich deep violet. Skin shadow is rosewood-plum, architectural shadow is indigo-violet, cloth shadow is aubergine. (3) HIGHLIGHTS: painted-in manually, NOT following physical light math exactly — highlight placement emphasizes the emotional reading of the form (the cheekbone lit even when key is behind the subject, the knuckle catching a gleam on the swing). (4) SURFACE SPECIFICITY: skin has painted-in blush and pore mark visible at close-up, not subsurface scattering. Hair reads as sculpted painted volume — individual strands are grouped into painted clumps, each clump receiving a single highlight stroke. Fabric shows woven texture painted, not cloth-sim. (5) ENVIRONMENT: chunky mid-poly architecture with surface painting of grime strata, rust oxidation pattern, and iridescent oil-slick streaks on wet stone — a deep-industrial undercity aesthetic of chemical-district grime, never any named franchise district. Every metal surface carries thin-film interference rainbow spec. Background compresses into painterly matte with visible brushwork — never crisp photographic detail, never clean CGI surface. (6) POST: Vision3 500T film stock emulation — warm-highlight / cool-shadow curve, halation blooming on any pixel above 90% luminance (light sources bloom into surrounding haze), subtle chromatic aberration at high-contrast silhouette edges, 35mm-equivalent grain floor present but refined. IMPERATIVE: FORTICHE PAINTERLY-3D HYBRID. 3D forms with hand-painted surface shading, 2D-animated FX layer on top. FORBID: flat cel shading, clean untextured CGI, photoreal live-action light math, standard anime eye geometry, pure black.
world.camera: 50mm eye-level medium view, dominant subject isolated against deliberate negative space
world.motionGrammar: Deliberate 24fps with hand-animated painted smear frames at peak action — 2-3 consecutive frames with painted directional motion streak visible as brushwork, not motion-blur. 1.5-2 second hold on emotional beat — character studies the situation, eyes tracking. Slow-in / slow-out weight. Momentum carries: a punch's aftershock ripples through cloth and hair for 4-6 frames after impact. No motion blur — smears are painted.

refDNAs.name: Fortiche Painted Texture Grammar
refDNAs.directive: Stylized animation frame in the Fortiche Production painterly-3D doctrine ( season 1 and 2). Medium/era: 2021–2024 Fortiche hybrid — 3D-modeled forms surface-shaded with oil-paint texture hand-applied at every silhouette edge. Named anchor: Fortiche impasto 2D-over-3D surface treatment — NOT anime, NOT cel — paint stroke IS the shading method. Signature light: motivated single-direction key; bounce fill in complementary hue (if key is teal, fill is magenta-pink; if key is warm amber, fill is cool violet); rim light is always an accent color, NEVER white; hard-black ambient occlusion forbidden — AOcclusion is painterly-soft in shadow-hue, not black. Color/grade: Fortiche Vision3 500T-derived curve — warm-highlight, cool-shadow, painterly halation on hot pixels; deep-violet for the darkest shadow value (NO pure black anywhere); magenta-pink + teal two-color system in the painted shadows and rims. Lens/optics: 35–50mm, f/2.8–f/4; Vision3 500T halation on specular and emissive sources; 35mm-equivalent grain present in every value step, not in the highlights only. Texture/render: visible brush stroke at every silhouette edge; skin has hand-painted blush and pore work, NOT subsurface-scattering smooth; hair masses show individual paint-stroke separation, not smooth spec; highlights are painted-in decisions, not PBR physical math. Composition+motion: deliberate 24fps with hand-animated painted smear frames at peak action (2–3 frames, motion-streak painted); 1.5–2s emotional hold; slow-in slow-out with weight; no motion-blur.
refDNAs.use: Applies Fortiche's impasto 2D-over-3D surface grammar — painted edges, violet shadow, complementary-hue fill, halation — as a texture and light layer over the chosen world's forms.
refDNAs.avoid: NO named franchise characters, NO iconic costumes or emblems, NO named powers, techniques, or signature moves, NO recognizable franchise locations; original subjects only.

refDNAs.name: Undercity Value-Tension Grammar
refDNAs.directive: Stylized animation frame in the Fortiche painterly-3D undercity-tension lineage (painted-3D grammar, original subjects only). Medium/era: Fortiche impasto 2D-over-3D, industrial-undercity register. Named anchor: undercity value-tension grammar — the deep-industrial chemical-district atmosphere of grime, oxidation, and iridescent oil-slick, held in painterly value contrast. Signature light: motivated single-direction key from a warm industrial lantern or a cool exterior, complementary-hue bounce, a jewel-tone accent rim; halation blooms on practicals; painterly-soft warm AO, no hard black. Color/grade: Fortiche warm-highlight/cool-shadow curve, deep-violet darkest shadow (no pure black), magenta-and-teal rim system; a grime palette of rust-orange, oxidized copper, and oil-slick iridescence on wet stone. Lens/optics: 35-50mm, f/2.8-f/4, halation on emissive sources, refined 35mm grain in every value step, 1-2px chromatic aberration at high-contrast silhouettes. Texture/render: visible oil-paint brush stroke at every silhouette, chunky mid-poly industrial architecture surface-painted with grime strata and thin-film rainbow spec on metal; painterly matte background. Composition+motion: deliberate 24fps with painted smear frames at peak, momentum carrying through cloth and hair after impact, a 1.5-2s value-charged emotional hold; the industrial value-tension is the mood.
refDNAs.use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers Fortiche's industrial-undercity painterly value-tension and grime-surface grammar onto the chosen world; use for a gritty, premium-stylized, industrial-atmosphere beat.
refDNAs.avoid: NO named franchise characters, NO iconic costumes or emblems, NO named powers, techniques, or signature moves, NO recognizable franchise locations; original subjects only.

refDNAs.name: Bridge Scale Grammar
refDNAs.directive: Stylized animation frame in the Fortiche painterly-3D monumental-scale lineage (painted-3D grammar, original subjects only). Medium/era: Fortiche impasto 2D-over-3D, grand-architecture register. Named anchor: painterly bridge-scale grammar — the monumental ornate structure spanning a divide, painterly grandeur against atmospheric depth. Signature light: motivated key raking the vast structure, warm practical lanterns and cool sky in balance, halation on the light sources, painterly-soft AO in the architectural depth. Color/grade: Fortiche warm-highlight/cool-shadow curve, deep-violet shadow (no black), jewel-tone accent rim; a grand palette of warm-stone ornament against a cool-atmospheric sky. Lens/optics: 35-50mm to wide, f/4-f/5.6 deep enough to hold the architecture, atmospheric painterly depth compressing the far span into brushwork; halation on distant lights. Texture/render: visible brush stroke at silhouettes, chunky mid-poly ornate architecture surface-painted with grime and gilt detail, painterly matte far-ground, thin-film spec on metal ornament. Composition+motion: monumental scale composition — a figure small against a vast ornate span, the structure filling the frame, slow reveal of grandeur; a painterly smear on any motion, a held awe-of-scale beat.
refDNAs.use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers Fortiche's monumental painterly-architecture scale grammar onto the chosen world; use for a grand, awe-of-scale, ornate-structure establishing beat.
refDNAs.avoid: NO named franchise characters, NO iconic costumes or emblems, NO named powers, techniques, or signature moves, NO recognizable franchise locations; original subjects only.

draft.previewPrompt: Cinematic stylized bed, 70-90 BPM route-dependent; analog synth pads, low drones, bowed cello or processed taiko reserved for value turns, ONE designed pressure swell for the main reveal. Space: wide painted hall. Arc: tension intro, value-shift build, single committed peak, dark warm resolve. Always: no vocals unless requested, duck under dialogue, act as a Foley/Sound Designer, exclude trailer brass, EDM drops, busy percussion clipping the VO, genre drift.
